pattern: Splitting on the single space delimiter and comparing each word's last character to the next word's first character -- wrapping the final comparison back to the first word -- checks the whole loop in one linear pass with no extra data structure.
hints: Split the sentence on spaces to get the list of words in order.
For every consecutive pair of words, the first word's last letter must match the second word's first letter -- check that for every adjacent pair, including nothing special yet for the wrap-around.
Don't forget the sentence is circular: the very last word's last letter must also match the very first word's first letter, closing the loop back to the start.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, four words forming a full loop
statement example, single word that loops to itself
statement example, breaks on the very first pair
single letter word, trivially loops to itself
every adjacent pair matches except the wrap-around from last word back to first
case sensitivity matters -- uppercase and lowercase of the same letter do not match
two words where only the wrap-around link needs checking
many short words all sharing the same repeated letter, an easy false positive for a buggy check

stress.description: randomly generated sentences, mixing chains that are circular by construction with ones that are deliberately broken
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    letters = string.ascii_lowercase
    word_count = rng.randint(1, min(max(size // 20, 1), 50))
    words = []
    last_char = rng.choice(letters)
    first_char_of_sentence = last_char
    for i in range(word_count):
        length = rng.randint(1, 10)
        middle = ''.join(rng.choice(letters) for _ in range(max(0, length - 2)))
        end_char = rng.choice(letters)
        word = last_char + middle + end_char
        words.append(word)
        last_char = end_char
    if rng.randint(0, 1) == 0 and word_count > 1:
        break_idx = rng.randint(0, word_count - 1)
        w = list(words[break_idx])
        w[-1] = rng.choice([c for c in letters if c != w[-1]])
        words[break_idx] = ''.join(w)
    sentence = ' '.join(words)
    return [sentence]
